Exploring Greece supports road access and pebble/small-pebble seabed for the first Koulouki beach. Confidence promoted to high; windProfile, scoring, coordinates and photos were not changed.

sourceNotes: Phase 2 Leros low cleanup 2026-05-28: Exploring Greece supports Kokkini as a Leros beach reached by road/dirt road from the Lakki side, with fine sand/sand, pebbles, shallow depth, no organized setup, and some tamarisk/pine shade. No wind, safety, or live condition claim was encoded.

Next popular-area confidence pass 2026-06-02: Exploring Greece directly supports Kokkini on Leros with road/dirt-road access, fine sand/sand beach, sandy-pebbly seabed, shallow depth, non-organized status, and normal accessibility; OSM corroborates the stored location. No windProfile, scoring, photos, or live calm/protected/safe claim was changed.

sourceNotes: Popular regions source pass 2026-06-02 B: LerosIsland directly supports Vromolithos near Panteli Bay with shingle/pebbly beach character, access directions, parking near Paradisos tavern and umbrellas/sunbeds on the north side. Didim Sea Lines separately supports Vromolithos as organized with umbrellas/sundecks, sandy north/south parts, pebbles over most of the beach and tavernas around. Coordinates, windProfile, scoring, photos, and live calm/protected/safe claims were not changed.

sourceNotes: Phase 2 Batch 3K source check 2026-05-25: AllOverGreece gives matching coordinates for Kryfos and describes access by trail/boat, including a rocky uneven hike from Panagies near Alinda or boat from Agia Marina. Corrected from strict boat-only to difficult path/boat wording.

Island confidence pass 2026-06-05: Leros records promoted to high for static beach facts only. Existing direct Leros island, Lerosinfo, Leros municipality and AllOverGreece source notes support identity/location plus recorded access, terrain, shade and service context. No coordinates, windProfile, scoring, photos, water-quality, or live calm/protected/safe claims were changed.

sourceNotes: Popular regions source pass 2026-06-02: AllOverGreece supports Blefoutis as soft sand/fine pebbles with tamarisk shade, easy access, parking, tavern and a small organized section; Aegean Islands separately supports fine pebbles, natural tamarisk shade and tavernas. Coordinates, windProfile, scoring, photos, and live calm/protected/safe claims were not changed.
